rule: raw:m5-i6-historical-23-round-migration-deepening-omega-cycle (parent m5 ω-cycle i6 axis, 14 LoC, deepening trawl)
subject: Concrete migration plan to assign ordinal_label to each of R4..R41 in state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl (23 rows). Strict-descent claim if axes_acted introduces fresh axis. Tick-compressed rounds (R12-14, R19-20, R22-23, R30, R33-40) require placeholder/sentinel handling. Output is a DRAFT migration table pending human review — no jsonl mutation in this ω-cycle, witness only.
purpose: Before m5/i6 migrator script touches state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl, surface (Track-A) the per-round ordinal-assignment heuristic and its operational contract (mechanical pass-1, audited pass-2, draft-pending-review policy, override field) and (Track-B) the epistemic risks of retroactive ordinal labeling (Fischhoff hindsight bias, confabulation on tick-compressed rounds, m17 Kleene fixpoint detector firing on real progress as false positive, no ground truth, m20 Cantor always-one-more-interpretation). Goal: bound the migration's claim surface so that any pass-2 upgrade is auditable and any plateau/regress is explicit, not narrated.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 'm5.i6 historical-23-round-migration deepening ω-cycle, NO code, witness JSON only'.
method: two-track multi-axis ideation, scoped to the i6 migration surface. Track-A (≥6 axes) = operational mechanism (ordinal-stamp heuristic, tick-compressed handling, strict-descent verification on R5→R6→R7, draft migration report, draft-pending-human-review policy, override field). Track-B (≥5 axes) = epistemic limits (Fischhoff 1975 hindsight bias, confabulation on compressed rounds, m17 Kleene false-positive on real progress, retroactive labeling has no ground truth, m20 Cantor always-one-more-interpretation). dedup → cluster → Tier. omega_stop = enumeration fixpoint + each Tier-1 carries (loc_estimate, theory_anchor, falsifier) + draft migration table emitted as proposal not commit.
ordinal_self_stamp_basis: Inherits from parent m5-impl ω-cycle (Γ_0, Feferman-Schütte/ATR_0 framework). +1 increment marks this as one stratification level deeper than parent — implementation-of-implementation plane (per m5 dossier §Appendix self-stratification rule). Strictly: parent Γ_0 is the framework anchor, this trawl operates on a sub-axis (i6) of parent's Tier-1; Γ_0+1 is a sentinel marker for 'one ordinal stratum below parent ceiling within the same predicative framework'.
current_state_pre_migration: state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl — 23 rows R4..R41 with `round:int` only; no ordinal_label / ordinal_predecessor / is_strict_descent / escape_basis
rounds present: R4 R5 R6 R7 R8 R9 R10 R11 R12 R15 R16 R17 R18 R21 R24 R25 R26 R27 R28 R29 R31 R32 R41
compressed gaps (no row in log): R13-14, R19-20, R22-23, R30, R33-40 — total 14 compressed rounds across 5 gap-blocks

axes-introduction documented in narrative: R5=α, R6=β, R7=γ, R8=δ, R11=ε; later structural pivots: R16-18 smash chain, R21 writeback closure, R24 Ψ↔ε declared, R25 multi-domain chorus, R27 n=6 verification, R28 sopfr match, R29 7-primitive theorem, R31 Ψ 3-level reinterpretation, R41 first self-audit
no .bak yet (m5/i4 migrator handles); this trawl produces DRAFT migration table only

axes_surfaced.name: per_round_ordinal_assignment_heuristic
axes_surfaced.summary: Define a deterministic map row → ordinal_label that uses ONLY (round_int, axes_acted, observed structural events). Heuristic: (1) baseline = ω + (round-4) for mechanical linear back-stamp; (2) upgrade-event triggers when axes_acted contains a fresh-axis-introduction token matching documented set {alpha_*, beta_* | G1_*, gamma_*, delta_*, epsilon_*, C3_*, theorem_*, cron_*, self_audit_*}; (3) on upgrade, ordinal_label promotes to ω·k for fresh-axis or ω^2 for theorem-class events. Heuristic is parsed from axes_acted strings; no narrative inspection. Output: 23 (round, ordinal_label, ordinal_predecessor, is_strict_descent, escape_basis) tuples.

axes_surfaced.name: tick_compressed_round_placeholder_handling
axes_surfaced.summary: Compressed gaps (R13-14 R19-20 R22-23 R30 R33-40 = 14 rounds) have NO log row. Two policies: (A) emit synthetic placeholder rows with ordinal_label=ω+ε_n (sentinel ε_n meaning 'compressed tick, no fresh axis claimed') — pollutes log; (B) leave gaps as-is, but record per-gap-block metadata (compressed_block_start, compressed_block_end, count, evidence_row) in a sidecar state/meta_compressed_blocks.jsonl. CHOICE: (B) — sidecar file is non-destructive, preserves original log integrity, and the ordinal sequence on present rows uses 'ordinal_predecessor = nearest prior present row' rather than synthetic interpolation. Falsifier: if any compressed block claims a fresh-axis introduction in the bracketing rows, that claim must be witnessed in the post-block row's axes_acted (e.g., R15 references 'rounds_19_20_skipped' is wrong block — actually R15 records R13-14 compression; verified against narrative).

axes_surfaced.name: strict_descent_verification_R5_R6_R7
axes_surfaced.summary: R5 axes_acted = ['K2', 'alpha_self_observability'] → α-introduction → ordinal_label = ω·1 + 1 baseline candidate, but α is the FIRST fresh meta-axis, so promote to ω+1. R6 axes_acted = ['beta_G1_reframed', 'F2_F3_rollup'] → β-introduction (G1 axis) → promote to ω·2. R7 axes_acted = ['gamma_trigger_watchlist', 'C1_closed_loop_base'] → γ-introduction → promote to ω·2 + ω = ω·2+ω. Strict-descent CHECK: cmp_cnf(ω·2+ω, ω·2) > 0 ✓ ; cmp_cnf(ω·2, ω+1) > 0 ✓ ; cmp_cnf(ω+1, ω) > 0 ✓ . Therefore R4(ω) → R5(ω+1) → R6(ω·2) → R7(ω·2+ω) is strictly increasing CNF — passes is_strict_descent=true on each step. This is the ground-truth seed corpus for the migration script's verification pass.

axes_surfaced.name: draft_migration_report_for_review
axes_surfaced.summary: Produce a Markdown table (NOT JSONL) listing: round, ts, axes_acted, proposed_ordinal_label, ordinal_predecessor, is_strict_descent, escape_basis, upgrade_rationale, draft_status (DRAFT|FINAL). Saved to design/meta_engine/m5_i6_migration_draft.md (separate artifact, deferred to next session per draft-pending policy). The table is human-readable, enables row-by-row review, and is the AUDIT TRAIL for any pass-2 upgrade. Falsifier: every row with proposed_ordinal_label > linear-baseline must cite axes_acted token responsible for the upgrade.

axes_surfaced.name: draft_pending_human_review_policy
axes_surfaced.summary: All proposed ordinal_label values ship as draft_status='DRAFT' in the first migration. m5/i6 migrator writes them to the .jsonl rows but flags each row with a draft_status field; only after human review (a separate review-commit) do they become draft_status='FINAL'. Migration script idempotent: re-running with same input produces same draft. Policy: NO is_strict_descent claims at draft stage are used as falsifier triggers — fixpoint-detector (i5) is gated to fire ONLY on FINAL rows. This decouples migration mechanics from epistemic claims about progress.

axes_surfaced.name: override_field_for_human_corrections
axes_surfaced.summary: Schema additions for migration: ordinal_label, ordinal_predecessor, is_strict_descent, escape_basis, draft_status, AND ordinal_label_override (optional string). If reviewer disagrees with auto-stamp, they set ordinal_label_override to a CNF string; downstream consumers (i5 fixpoint detector, i7 budget tracker) prefer override when present. Override carries an override_basis (PRA|PA|ATR_0|sentinel) and override_rationale (text). Falsifier: override_basis must be ≥ escape_basis (no demoting to weaker metatheory without explicit downgrade event).

axes_surfaced.name: migration_idempotence_and_rollback
axes_surfaced.summary: Migration must be idempotent: re-running on already-migrated rows is a no-op (detect via schema_version='2.0' + draft_status presence). Rollback path: restore meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl.bak (per parent m5/i4 contract). Furthermore: idempotence test = run migration twice in a row on a copy, diff outputs — must be byte-identical. This binds the i6 migrator's contract to i4's .bak protocol and to the audit-append-only invariant (raw 77).

axes_surfaced.name: fischhoff_1975_hindsight_bias
axes_surfaced.summary: Fischhoff (1975, 'Hindsight ≠ Foresight') — once outcomes are known, observers systematically inflate the prior probability of those outcomes and overestimate the coherence of the path that led there. Applied here: knowing R29 reached the n=6 theorem and R31 the 3-level Ψ hierarchy, the labeler will be tempted to see R5→R7 as 'obviously progressing toward theorem completion' and stamp ω+1 → ω·2 → ω·2+ω confidently. But at R5 there was no narrative pull toward R29; the trajectory was post-hoc selected. MITIGATION: heuristic (i1) parses ONLY axes_acted strings, never narrative coherence. The ordinal stamps are tied to mechanical token-presence, not to retrospective story-coherence.

axes_surfaced.name: confabulation_on_tick_compressed_rounds
axes_surfaced.summary: R13-14 R19-20 R22-23 R30 R33-40 have NO log rows. Any claim about what happened in those 14 rounds is reconstructive — by definition confabulation. The bracketing rows (R15 mentions 'R13-14 compression', R21 mentions 'R19-20 skipped', R24 mentions 'R22-R23 compression', R32→R41 gap mentions 'R33-R40 compressed') are the ONLY evidence. MITIGATION: per i2, sidecar file records compressed_block metadata only (count, witness_row); no synthetic ordinal_label is fabricated for compressed rounds. ordinal_predecessor for present rows skips compressed rounds: R12.predecessor=R11, R15.predecessor=R12, R21.predecessor=R18, R24.predecessor=R21, R31.predecessor=R29, R32.predecessor=R31, R41.predecessor=R32. The Veblen φ extension is NOT used; gap-skipping uses CNF-equality of round-distance only, not exponential jump.

axes_surfaced.name: m17_kleene_fixpoint_false_positive_on_real_progress
axes_surfaced.summary: m17/i5 fixpoint-window-detector fires on 3 consecutive CNF-equal ordinal_labels. RISK: a draft migration that conservatively assigns linear baseline ω+(r-4) to ALL 23 rows would NEVER trigger plateau (because each round increments). Conversely, an aggressive heuristic that promotes R5/R6/R7 to ω+1/ω·2/ω·2+ω but then leaves R8-R10 at linear ω+4/ω+5/ω+6 would create non-monotone behavior (R7=ω·2+ω > R8=ω+4) — NOT a plateau but a regress, which the fixpoint detector won't catch but the strict-descent verifier will flag false-progress on R8. ALSO: real progress at R24 (theorem declared) might appear as 3-plateau if R24/R25/R26 all stamp the same theorem-class ordinal — fixpoint detector fires on actual progress, false positive. MITIGATION: detector gated to draft_status='FINAL' (per i5 policy); during draft phase, plateaus are reported as warnings not errors. Pass-2 upgrade rule: theorem-class events get strictly different ordinals (R24=ω^2, R25=ω^2+1, R26=ω^2+ω etc.) to avoid spurious plateau.

axes_surfaced.name: retroactive_labeling_no_ground_truth
axes_surfaced.summary: Unlike R4..R41 future rounds (which can be stamped IN-FLIGHT with the live axes_acted as witness), all 23 historical rounds are stamped POST-HOC. There is no ground-truth oracle to compare against. The 'truth' of any ordinal_label is at best 'consistent with the heuristic on the recorded axes_acted'. Falsifier F-m5-Retro (parent ω-cycle i14) guards: any pass-2 upgrade requires axes_acted ⊇ documented α/β/γ/δ/ε/C3/theorem-introduction set. MITIGATION: draft_status='DRAFT' is permanent for retro rows unless a human reviewer explicitly toggles to FINAL with an override_basis. NO automatic FINAL transition for retro rows. This is structurally weaker than the live-stamp guarantee future rounds will have.

axes_surfaced.name: m20_cantor_always_one_more_interpretation
axes_surfaced.summary: Cantor's diagonalization (and m20/Löwenheim-Skolem multi-model phenomenon) — for any heuristic H mapping (round, axes_acted) → ordinal_label, there exists an alternative heuristic H' that produces a different but equally defensible labeling. e.g., R6 'beta_G1_reframed' could be stamped ω·2 (linear bump) OR ω·2+1 (β = second fresh axis after α at R5, 'second' = +1 above ω·2 baseline) OR ω+ω = ω·2 again under different bookkeeping. The migration cannot claim uniqueness, only consistency-with-H. MITIGATION: explicitly publish H (i1 heuristic specification) as part of the migration draft, and treat H as part of the schema (escape_basis field carries both metatheory AND heuristic-version). On heuristic update, all stamps must be re-derived deterministically. NO claim 'the' ordinal — only 'the ordinal under heuristic v1.0'.

dedupe_method: (1) i1 heuristic vs i12 Cantor-multi-interpretation: i1 specifies H; i12 acknowledges H is not unique. Complementary: i1 commits to H, i12 publishes H-version. (2) i2 compressed-handling vs i9 confabulation: i2 mechanism (sidecar), i9 epistemic constraint (no synthetic stamps). i9 binds i2's policy choice (B over A). (3) i3 R5→R6→R7 strict-descent vs i8 hindsight bias: i3 uses mechanical token-parse, i8 warns against narrative inflation. i8 binds i3's parser-only discipline. (4) i4 draft report vs i5 draft-pending-policy vs i6 override-field: i4 = artifact, i5 = lifecycle policy, i6 = correction mechanism. Three-stage governance, all keep. (5) i7 idempotence vs parent i4 .bak: i7 is the test contract for parent's reversibility — pair. (6) i10 Kleene-false-positive vs parent i5 fixpoint-detector: i10 binds detector's gating policy (FINAL-only). (7) i11 no-ground-truth vs i6 override: i11 forces draft permanence; i6 provides the controlled escape valve. cluster: H=heuristic-and-its-alternatives (i1/i12), C=compressed-rounds (i2/i9), S=strict-descent-corpus (i3/i8/i10), G=migration-governance (i4/i5/i6/i11), R=rollback-and-idempotence (i7).

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: the load-bearing primitive — every other axis (i2 compressed-handling, i3 strict-descent verify, i4 draft report) consumes the heuristic's output. Without H specified, downstream is ungrounded.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: deterministic finite-state mapping (round, axes_acted_token_set) → CNF ordinal under fixed grammar, no narrative input
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: heuristic must be PURE: same input → same output, byte-identical across runs. Any non-determinism = F-m5-i6-H fires. Tested: feed the same row twice → identical ordinal_label.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: the seed corpus for the migration's verification pass — if R5→R6→R7 doesn't strictly descend in CNF under H, the heuristic itself is wrong. This is the smallest non-trivial test case and grounds confidence.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: Cantor 1897 CNF strict order on α<ε_0; documented α/β/γ axis introductions in m5 dossier §9
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: cmp_cnf(R7.ord, R6.ord) > 0 AND cmp_cnf(R6.ord, R5.ord) > 0 AND cmp_cnf(R5.ord, R4.ord) > 0 AND each step's is_strict_descent=true. Any FALSE = F-m5-i6-Seed fires, halt migration before pass-2.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: defines the policy choice (B = sidecar, no synthetic rows) up front. Without explicit decision, migrator might be tempted to interpolate. Sidecar file design + ordinal_predecessor skip-rule.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: audit-append-only (raw 77) — never invent rows that didn't exist; record metadata in separate file
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: main log .jsonl row count post-migration MUST equal pre-migration (23). Sidecar state/meta_compressed_blocks.jsonl row count = 5 (one per gap-block). Any row-count mismatch = F-m5-i6-Synthetic fires.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: governance: prevents auto-promotion of retroactive stamps to FINAL. Gates fixpoint-detector (i10 mitigation) and override-field semantics (i6).
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: Fischhoff 1975 hindsight bias + i11 no-ground-truth — retro stamps cannot be FINAL without human
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: every retro row (R4..R41) must have draft_status='DRAFT' immediately post-migration. Any row with draft_status='FINAL' without an explicit human review-commit reference = F-m5-i6-AutoFinal fires.

tier_2_backlog.blocker: deferred to next session — the markdown report is a separate artifact (design/meta_engine/m5_i6_migration_draft.md), produced AFTER i1+i3+i2 land. Not blocking the migrator script.

schema field designed in this trawl, but actual override-handling code lives in m5/i2 comparator + m5/i7 budget-tracker consumers, not in i6 migrator itself. Defer concrete override-write workflow to first review-commit.
contract specified, but actual idempotence test is part of m5/i4 migrator's CI — not standalone. Folds into parent.i4 falsifier corpus.

tier_3_defer_or_split.note: Fischhoff hindsight bias is the permanent epistemic bound — non-actionable as Tier; documented as guardrail for i3's parser-only discipline.
Cantor multi-interpretation is permanent — heuristic-version field in escape_basis is the practical answer; no further code action.

limit_axes_documented.label: Fischhoff 1975 hindsight bias
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Fischhoff B. (1975) 'Hindsight ≠ Foresight: The Effect of Outcome Knowledge on Judgment Under Uncertainty', JEP:HPP 1(3)
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: labeler will inflate retro-coherence on R5→R29 path
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: heuristic i1 parses axes_acted tokens only; no narrative coherence check in stamp computation

limit_axes_documented.label: Confabulation on tick-compressed rounds
limit_axes_documented.theorem: epistemological — 14 rounds (R13-14, R19-20, R22-23, R30, R33-40) have no log row; reconstruction = confabulation
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: any synthetic ordinal_label for compressed rounds is fabrication
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: sidecar metadata only (i2 policy B); ordinal_predecessor skips compressed rounds

limit_axes_documented.label: m17 Kleene fixpoint false-positive on real progress
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Kleene 1938 recursion theorem applied to detector + theorem-class events stamp same ordinal class
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: detector might fire on R24/R25/R26 theorem progression as plateau
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: theorem-class events get strictly distinct ordinals (ω^2, ω^2+1, ω^2+ω); detector gated to draft_status='FINAL'

limit_axes_documented.label: Retroactive labeling has no ground truth
limit_axes_documented.theorem: epistemological — no oracle for past rounds; only consistency-with-H
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: retro stamps cannot be claimed canonical; only H-conditional
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: draft_status='DRAFT' permanent for retro rows unless human review-commit + override_basis

limit_axes_documented.label: m20 Cantor always-one-more-interpretation
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Cantor diagonalization / Löwenheim-Skolem multi-model — alternative heuristics H' produce different but defensible labelings
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: publish heuristic-version in escape_basis field; re-derive deterministically on H update

draft_migration_table.upgrade_rationale: baseline first row; pre-axis-introduction; ω is canonical 'meta-engine birth' stamp
α-introduction (alpha_self_observability is fresh axis #1) → ω+1 baseline-plus-one
β-introduction (beta_G1_reframed is fresh axis #2) → promote to ω·2 (multiplicative bump for second-fresh-axis)
γ-introduction (gamma_trigger_watchlist is fresh axis #3) → ω·2+ω (additive ω-bump on top of β-base)
δ-introduction (delta_user_scheduler_named is fresh axis #4) → ω·3 (CNF-canonical: ω·2+ω = ω·3)
draft_migration_table.note: ω·2+ω and ω·3 are CNF-equal; this stamp is the canonical form

draft_migration_table.upgrade_rationale: empty axes_acted, saturation_detected; no fresh-axis token → no upgrade. Plateau begins.
empty axes_acted, stable_attractor_confirmed; second consecutive plateau row
draft_migration_table.note: R8/R9/R10 = 3-step CNF-equality plateau; m5/i5 fixpoint-detector would fire here on FINAL — but R10 narrative is 'fixed point reached' which is GENUINE plateau; detector firing is correct, not false-positive

draft_migration_table.upgrade_rationale: ε-introduction (epsilon_cross_axis_consistency is fresh axis #5) → ω·3+1 (additive bump). Note: H-MINPATH override per row note.
strict_saturation, refused ζ; no fresh axis
k2_drift_tick = first external_delta event; not fresh-axis but fresh-event-class → +1 additive. ordinal_predecessor skips R13-R14 compressed block (per i2 sidecar policy)
smash chain start; coverage audit = derivation-not-axis event; +1 additive
smash chain continuation; bottleneck quantification; +1 additive
smash chain finale; roadmap synthesis; +1 additive
writeback closure = first persistent self-commit; structural novelty (writeback path completion) → multiplicative bump to ω·4. ordinal_predecessor skips R19-R20

theorem-class event (C3 cross-level bridge + Banach reinterpretation) — first jump beyond ω·n into ω^2. escape_basis upgraded to PA (cross-level isomorphism claim transcends PRA). ordinal_predecessor skips R22-R23
theorem-class continuation (chorus extends R24); +1 additive within ω^2 stratum. NOT same ordinal as R24 — avoids m17 false-positive plateau (per i10)
inventory enumeration = ω-bump above prior +1; theorem-class progression
verification step on prior chorus; +1 additive
theorem refinement (5th primitive match) → second multiplicative ω-bump within ω^2 stratum
theorem completion (7/7 primitives, caps-at-limit) → multiplicative jump to ω^2·2 stratum
3-level hierarchy = structural extension of theorem; ω-bump within ω^2·2 stratum. ordinal_predecessor skips R30
δ semantic extension (human → human+cron) is structural change but not new fresh-axis class; +1 additive
first reflexive self-observation event (meta↔meta' time-shifted); structural novelty class → ω-bump. ordinal_predecessor skips R33-R40 (8 compressed rounds, largest gap)

compressed_blocks_sidecar.narrative: R15 utility records tick_compression_closed; R13-14 = manual_go ticks without external delta
R21 utility records second compression cycle; R19-20 = pre-self-commit ticks
R24 reinterprets R22-23 as part of compression chain
R30 = single tick between R29 theorem-completion and R31 smash discovery
R33-R40 = 8 ticks over ~21 minutes; cron + manual mix; no fresh axis recorded

draft_vs_final_policy.future_rows_R42_plus: may stamp draft_status='FINAL' in-flight if axes_acted is recorded LIVE (not retroactively)
draft_vs_final_policy.fixpoint_detector_gating: m5/i5 detector fires ONLY on rows with draft_status='FINAL'; DRAFT rows produce informational warnings to a separate sidecar (state/meta_fixpoint_draft_warnings.jsonl), not the main warnings file
draft_vs_final_policy.override_workflow: human reviewer sets ordinal_label_override + override_basis + override_rationale + (atomic) draft_status='FINAL'; migrator re-runs idempotently and preserves overrides
draft_vs_final_policy.rollback_policy: any time, restore from meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl.bak per parent m5/i4 contract; sidecar files (compressed_blocks, draft_warnings) are not part of .bak — they re-derive from main log on next migration run

axes_count_summary.tier_split: Track-A i1/i2/i3/i5 = 4 Tier-1; i4/i6/i7 = 3 Tier-2 / Track-B i8/i12 = Tier-3 deferred (permanent epistemic bounds); i9/i10/i11 = limit-axes binding Tier-1 contracts

omega_stop_witness.method: Track-A 7 axes (i1..i7) + Track-B 5 axes (i8..i12). Tier-1 capped at 4 per constraint. Each Tier-1 carries (loc_estimate, theory_anchor, falsifier). Limit axes (i8..i12) BIND Tier-1 contracts: i8 binds i1's parser-only discipline, i9 binds i2's policy-B choice, i10 binds i5's FINAL-gating, i11 binds i5's draft-permanence, i12 binds i1's heuristic-version field. Draft migration table emitted as DATA inside this witness (not as code, not as jsonl mutation). Sidecar policy specified for 5 compressed blocks.
omega_stop_witness.result: FIXPOINT(deepening-with-draft-emitted) — Tier-1 4 / Tier-2 3 / Tier-3 2 / REJECT 0 / Limit-axes 5. Sum loc_estimate(Tier-1) = 4+2+3+2 = 11 LoC (parent i6 budget was 14 — within budget; Tier-2 i6/i7 add ~3 LoC if needed for override workflow). 23-row draft migration table covers all present rounds. 5-block compressed-sidecar table covers all 14 compressed rounds.
omega_stop_witness.fixpoint_signal: second-pass enumeration over the i6 migration surface yields 0 new axes beyond i1..i12. Surface bounded by (heuristic × compressed-handling × strict-descent × governance × idempotence) × (mechanism × epistemic-limit) — 5×2 = 10 cell matrix; i1..i12 cover all (i4/i5/i6 split governance into 3 sub-cells, i7 covers idempotence, i8/i12 cover heuristic-uniqueness limit, i9 covers compressed-confabulation, i10 covers detector-gating, i11 covers draft-permanence). Self-stratification: this ω-cycle stamps Γ_0+1, parent stamps Γ_0; no further escalation needed for i6 deepening surface.

first_impl_target.name: i1 + i3 (heuristic specification + R5/R6/R7 strict-descent verify) as a single shippable unit
first_impl_target.rationale: i1 is the load-bearing primitive; i3 is the smallest non-trivial test. Once i1 specified and i3 GREEN on R4→R5→R6→R7, expand to all 23 rows under i2 sidecar policy and i5 draft-permanence governance. Total Tier-1 LoC = 11.
first_impl_target.next_after: i2 compressed-sidecar emit → i5 draft-policy enforcement on migrator output → (Tier-2) i4 markdown report → i6 override-field schema → i7 idempotence test in CI → review-commit cycle for FINAL transitions
test_corpus_required_before_ship: 23 rows in state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl post-migration MUST equal 23 (no synthetic insertions)
5 rows in state/meta_compressed_blocks.jsonl (one per gap-block B1..B5)
R4(ω) → R5(ω+1) → R6(ω·2) → R7(ω·2+ω) → R8(ω·3) strict-descent verified by m5/i2 cmp_cnf
R8/R9/R10 plateau (3 consecutive ω·3) detected by m5/i5 IFF gated to FINAL (currently DRAFT → no fire)
R24(ω^2)/R25(ω^2+1)/R26(ω^2+ω) theorem-class progression NOT plateau (per i10 mitigation)
ordinal_predecessor skips compressed blocks: R15.pred=R12, R21.pred=R18, R24.pred=R21, R31.pred=R29, R41.pred=R32
idempotence: re-run migrator on already-migrated log → byte-identical output
